subject: UX & Research
title: Color Perception and Meaning
description: A tiny lesson with a big payoff: Color Perception and Meaning.

hook.text: Red means danger in the West, luck in China, and mourning in South Africa. Color meaning is cultural.

buildup.text: About 8% of men have some form of color blindness. Red-green is the most common type.

discovery.text: A form that marks errors only with red color is invisible to color-blind users. Add icons or text too.

twist.text: Even for sighted users, color alone carries weak info. A/B tests show that adding text labels lifts clarity 30%.

climax.text: Use color to reinforce meaning, never as the only signal. Pair it with shape, text, or position.

punchline.text: Color supports meaning. It should never be the only signal.

quiz.question: Why shouldn't errors be indicated by color alone?
options: About 8% of men are color-blind, so color-only signals are invisible to them
Colors load slowly on mobile devices
Red is considered lucky in some cultures
